title: Tender Polish Potato Toast
description: A fresh and tender Polish dish featuring new potatoes, perfect for the monsoon season, and diabetic-friendly. This recipe is a delicious and healthy twist on traditional Polish cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, toss the sliced potatoes with melted butter, salt, and pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
While the potatoes are roasting, toast the bread until it is lightly browned.
Once the potatoes are done, let them cool for a few minutes before topping each slice of toast with a few slices of potato, a sprinkle of chopped fresh dill, and a dollop of low-fat sour cream.
Top each slice of toast with a sprinkle of grated low-fat cheddar cheese.
Garnish with additional chopped fresh dill if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
